title: End-to-End E-commerce DWH
description: Unified 15+ fragmented data sources into a centralized Snowflake DWH using DBT and Airflow. Implemented SCD Type 2 tracking for inventory history.

detailedAnalysis.problem: The legacy e-commerce system suffered from extreme data fragmentation across 15+ isolated sources (ERP, CRM, Shopify, etc.). Reporting was manual, error-prone, and lagged by over 24 hours, making real-time inventory decisions impossible.
detailedAnalysis.solution: I architected a modern Medallion DWH (Bronze/Silver/Gold) on Snowflake. We utilized Airflow for robust orchestration and dbt for modular, test-driven transformations. A key feature was the implementation of dbt-expectations to gate data quality at the source layer.
detailedAnalysis.impact: The new architecture delivered a 95% reduction in reporting latency and eliminated 200+ redundant manual Excel reconciliations. This empowered the executive team with a 'Single Source of Truth' for global margin and inventory performance.

codeTeaser:
models:
  - name: fct_orders
    tests:
      - dbt_expectations.expect_column_values_to_be_between:
          column_name: order_date
          min_value: '2020-01-01'

challenges.solution: Unified 15+ sources into a centralized ODS layer.
Optimized Airflow tasks for incremental loading.
Integrated dbt-expectations for automated DQ testing.

mermaidCode:
graph LR
  subgraph Sources
    S1[ERP]
    S2[CRM]
    S3[E-comm]
  end
  Sources --> AF[Airflow]
  AF --> SF_RAW[RAW Layer]
  SF_RAW --> DBT[DBT Engine]
  DBT --> SF_GOLD[Gold Layer]
  SF_GOLD --> BI[Power BI]

title: Real-time Activity Streaming
description: Architected a Kafka-Flink pipeline processing 100k events/sec for real-time personalization engine. Achieved 500ms end-to-end latency.

detailedAnalysis.problem: The previous batch-based recommendation engine could only reflect user behavior from the prior day. This lack of real-time context led to low engagement and missed cross-sell opportunities during active sessions.
detailedAnalysis.solution: We deployed a Kafka-Flink pipeline that processes user events (clicks, views, carts) in real-time. By leveraging RocksDB state backends and custom watermark strategies, we maintained sub-second stateful consistency across ultra-high throughput streams.
detailedAnalysis.impact: Deployment of this real-time infrastructure directly powered the 'Dynamic Carousel,' resulting in a 12% increase in Click-Through Rate (CTR) and an immediate boost in per-session conversion metrics.

challenges.solution: Implemented clustered Kafka, partitioned topics, optimized configs.
Utilized Flink's RocksDB backend, incremental checkpointing.
Adopted Avro schemas with Confluent Schema Registry.

mermaidCode:
graph TD
  UA[User Activity] --> |100k ev/s| KAFKA[Kafka Cluster]
  KAFKA --> |Stream| FLINK[Flink Logic]
  subgraph Flink Cluster
    FLINK --> |Windowing| ST[State]
    ST --> |Low Latency| EN[Engine]
  end
  EN --> |Update| DB[Profile Store]

title: Automated Financial Ingestion
description: Secure, compliant ingestion engine for banking transactions. Automated 100% of audit logging and PII masking protocols.

detailedAnalysis.problem: Banking transactions were being ingested via sensitive manual SFTP processes. Compliance teams were overwhelmed with 10+ hours a week of manual PII audits and documentation requirements.
detailedAnalysis.solution: I developed a secure serverless ingestion engine using AWS Glue and Python. We automated PII masking via SHA-256 hashing at the landing zone and implemented immutable S3 audit logs using AWS KMS for encryption-at-rest.
detailedAnalysis.impact: The system successfully passed a Tier-1 banking audit with zero non-compliance findings. Manual audit effort was eliminated entirely, and data ingestion frequency was increased from daily to 15-minute intervals.

challenges.solution: Applied KMS encryption and automated PII masking in-transit.
Dynamic Glue worker allocation based on partition size.
Automated audit logs to immutable S3 bucket.

mermaidCode:
graph LR
  BANK[Banking API] --> |SFTP| S3_IN[S3 Landing]
  subgraph AWS Layer
    S3_IN --> GLUE[AWS Glue]
    GLUE --> |Masking| KMS[Security Hub]
  end
  GLUE --> |Parquet| S3_GOLD[Analytics Ready]

title: Scalable Data Quality Framework
description: Built a custom Python framework for proactive data validation using Great Expectations. Decreased upstream bugs by 75%.

detailedAnalysis.problem: Upstream data changes were frequently breaking downstream dashboards, causing executive 'trust lack' in reports. Bugs often went undetected for 24+ hours until a business user reported an anomaly.
detailedAnalysis.solution: We shifted 'Quality to the Left' by integrating Great Expectations into our CI/CD and Airflow pipelines. This created an automated gateway where all ingestion must pass 500+ granular validation points before loading.
detailedAnalysis.impact: Upstream bug frequency dropped by 75%. Automated 'Data Docs' now serve as a living contract between engineers and stakeholders, restoring organizational trust in the analytics layer.

challenges.solution: Centralized GX checkpoint registry across all pipelines.
Pre-deployment validation scripts for CI/CD integration.
Real-time Slack alerting with failure logs.

mermaidCode:
graph TD
  PIPE[Pipeline] --> |Check Point| GX[Great Expectations]
  subgraph DQ Hub
    GX --> |Validate| JSON[JSON Rules]
    JSON --> |Pass/Fail| RULES[Logic Gate]
  end
  RULES --> |Fail| SLACK[Slack Alert]
  RULES --> |Pass| LOAD[Ready]
